title_original: Disse fire ble nye sentre for kvanteteknologi
summary_original: Norwegian institutions, including Simula, University of Oslo, and Sintef, have been awarded 244 million Norwegian kroner by The Research Council of Norway to establish four quantum technology centers. Announced on December 17, 2025, these five-year centers focus on quantum sensors, quantum computing, quantum software, and quantum communication networks, in collaboration with international partners like the Niels Bohr Institute and Linköping University. The initiative is part of the government’s broader quantum program, “Kvantespranget,” totaling 1.1 billion Norwegian kroner over five years, with 100 new student positions. Norwegian ministers highlight this as a step toward national security, increased expertise, and competitiveness in the global quantum race, with broad implications for security, industry, and education.
